key_insight: Cepstral analysis makes Green-Kubo thermal conductivity calculations for MOFs much more stable, less parameter-sensitive, and faster to converge.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable, automation-ready prediction of heat transport in porous materials, supporting the design of MOFs for thermal management and gas-separation applications.
method: Run equilibrium molecular dynamics with Green-Kubo heat-flux autocorrelation analysis.
Apply cepstral analysis to denoise the signal and reduce sensitivity to arbitrary correlation-length choices.
Use machine-learned moment tensor potentials trained on DFT data for MOF-5, HKUST-1, and ZIF-8.
evidence_signal: Across three prototypical MOFs, the cepstral approach showed stable results over a wide range of correlation lengths and converged within about 1–2 ns of total sampling time, unlike conventional direct GK analysis.
watch_item: Performance still depends on the quality of the trained interatomic potential and the underlying MD sampling, so transferability should be checked for new MOFs and conditions.
paper_title: Cepstral Analysis to accelerate Green-Kubo thermal conductivity calculations of Metal-Organic Frameworks
